sections.paragraph:
pr.n. Samuel, [Shemuel] (according to 1Sa 1:20, i.q. שְׁמוּעֲאֵל “heard of God,” unless it be preferred “name of God,” taking שְׁמוּ as a sing. const. i.q. שֵׁם, compare רֵעַ and רְעוּ, מֵת and מְתוּ, פְּנוּ, פְּנִי i.q. פָּנֶה face [the Scripture derivation of a name must always be the true one]).-

(1) the name of a very celebrated judge and prophet of the Hebrews, the son of Elkanah, of the tribe of Ephraim ( 1Sa 1:1) [this is wholly incorrect; he was a Levite].-(2) Another of the same name, also the son of Elkanah, grandfather of Heman, 1Ch 6:13, 18 1Ch 6:18, who is mentioned among the Levites and singers. [But this is the same person as No. 1.]-(3) Num 34:24.-(4) 1Ch 7:2.
